feed poller, parser, transformer, cache\",\"description\":\"## What\\nBuild the core backend: Hono app that polls all 8 MTA GTFS-RT feeds every 30 seconds, parses protobuf (including NYCT extensions), transforms into StationArrivals model, and caches in memory.\\n\\n## Why\\nThis is the beating heart of the entire application. Every feature — arrivals, transfers, alerts, delay detection, trip tracking, train positions — depends on this pipeline running correctly every 30 seconds.\\n\\n## Subtasks\\n- [ ] Hono app setup (app.ts): routes, middleware, static asset serving config\\n- [ ] Feed poller (poller.ts): setInterval(30s), fetch all 8 feeds in parallel with Promise.allSettled\\n- [ ] Feed parser (parser.ts): protobuf decode using compiled proto, extract NYCT extensions\\n- [ ] Feed transformer (transformer.ts): convert parsed data → StationArrivals indexed by stopId\\n- [ ] In-memory cache (cache.ts): 30s TTL, fallback to last-good response on fetch failure, mark stale after 5 min\\n- [ ] Circuit breaker: after 3 consecutive failures on a feed, pause 60s before retrying\\n- [ ] API routes: GET /api/arrivals/:stationId, GET /api/stations, GET /api/stations/:id\\n- [ ] Health endpoint: GET /api/health with per-feed status\\n- [ ] Structured JSON logging on every poll (timestamp, feed, status, latency, entities, errors)\\n- [ ] Static asset serving: Hono serves /* from web dist directory, /api/* from routes\\n- [ ] Entry point (index.ts): start server, start poller, log startup info\\n\\n## Considerations\\n- Use Promise.allSettled (not Promise.all) so one feed failure doesn't block others\\n- Each feed has its own circuit breaker state — independent failure isolation\\n- The cache must serve stale data rather than nothing when feeds are down\\n- Per-feed timestamps must be tracked for the data freshness feature (Phase 7)\\n- Memory usage: ~8 feeds × ~500 entities × 30s = manageable in-memory, no external cache needed\\n- The poller should start polling immediately on startup (not wait 30s for first A single container on apexalgo-iad (us-east-1) serves both the Hono API backend and static React frontend through the existing Cloudflare Tunnel. The app provides real-time arrivals, configurable favorites, transfer intelligence, alerts, trip tracking, commute analytics, accessibility features, and system health visualization.\\n\\nThe value proposition is speed-to-information (favorites-first, data in under 3 seconds), transfer intelligence (real-time \\\"should I transfer?\\\" analysis), and alerts that are actually useful (plain-language, filtered to YOUR stations).\\n\\n## Architecture Decision\\nSingle container on apexalgo-iad was chosen over Cloudflare Workers or Fly.io because:\\n- Zero new infra cost (rides on existing cluster)\\n- us-east-1 gives sub-10ms latency to MTA feeds (NYC-hosted)\\n- Cloudflare Tunnel already configured for public HTTPS access\\n- GitOps deployment via ArgoCD follows existing patterns\\n- Same-origin eliminates CORS entirely\\n\\n## Progress\\n- [x] Phase 1: Core — Real-time arrivals, favorites, search, onboarding, complex mapping\\n- [x] Phase 2: Smart Commute — Transfer analysis, commute presets, express/local\\n- [x] Phase 3: Alerts & Notifications — Alert simplification, push notifications\\n- [x] Phase 4: Polish — Offline, accessibility, performance, error states\\n- [x] Phase 5: Intelligence — Predictive delays, trip tracking, context switching, journal\\n- [x] Phase 6: Awareness — Elevator status, walking comparison, OMNY, train diagram, health dashboard, annual summary\\n- [x] Phase 7: Trust & Resilience — Shuttle bus info, data freshness\\n- [x] Cross-cutting: Testing, security, data migration, observability\\n\\n## Migration Notes\\n- CI migrated from GitHub Actions to Argo Workflows (mta-my-way-ci WorkflowTemplate)\\n- All 8 phase beads (mta-my-way-s23.1 through mta-my-way-s23.8) closed\\n- CI pipeline runs lint (Biome + ESLint), typecheck (TypeScript), and tests (Vitest)\",\"design\":\"\",\"acceptance_criteria\":\"\",\"notes\":\"\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":0,\"issue_type\":\"genesis\",\"owner\":\"\",\"created_at\":\"2026-03-20T11:11:14.189414606Z\",\"created_by\":\"coding\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-07-03T19:02:45.312943286Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-07-03T19:02:45.312943286Z\",\"close_reason\":\"Completed\",\"source_system\":\"\",\"source_repo\":\".\",\"deleted_by\":\"\",\"delete_reason\":\"\",\"original_type\":\"\",\"compaction_level\":0,\"original_size\":0,\"sender\":\"\",\"labels\":[\"umbrella\"]}\n2\t{\"id\":\"mta-my-way-s23.1.5\",\"title\":\"Backend: feed poller, parser, transformer, cache\",\"description\":\"## What\\nBuild the core backend: Hono app that polls all 8 MTA GTFS-RT feeds every 30 seconds, parses protobuf (including NYCT extensions), transforms into StationArrivals model, and caches in memory.\\n\\n## Why\\nThis is the beating heart of the entire application. Every feature — arrivals, transfers, alerts, delay detection, trip tracking, train positions — depends on this pipeline running correctly every 30 seconds.\\n\\n## Subtasks\\n- [ ] Hono app setup (app.ts): routes, middleware, static asset serving config\\n- [ ] Feed poller (poller.ts): setInterval(30s), fetch all 8 feeds in parallel with Promise.allSettled\\n- [ ] Feed parser (parser.ts): protobuf decode using compiled proto, extract NYCT extensions\\n- [ ] Feed transformer (transformer.ts): convert parsed data → StationArrivals indexed by stopId\\n- [ ] In-memory cache (cache.ts): 30s TTL, fallback to last-good response on fetch failure, mark stale after 5 min\\n- [ ] Circuit breaker: after 3 consecutive failures on a feed, pause 60s before retrying\\n- [ ] API routes: GET /api/arrivals/:stationId, GET /api/stations, GET /api/stations/:id\\n- [ ] Health endpoint: GET /api/health with per-feed status\\n- [ ] Structured JSON logging on every poll (timestamp, feed, status, latency, entities, errors)\\n- [ ] Static asset serving: Hono serves /* from web dist directory, /api/* from routes\\n- [ ] Entry point (index.ts): start server, start poller, log startup info\\n\\n## Considerations\\n- Use Promise.allSettled (not Promise.all) so one feed failure doesn't block others\\n- Each feed has its own circuit breaker state — independent failure isolation\\n- The cache must
